entities.name: BIOCHAR
entities.description: A carbon-rich material produced from biomass used to improve soil fertility and agricultural productivity

entities.name: AVOCADO
entities.description: A fruit-bearing tree whose seeds are used as feedstock for biochar production

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the study on biochar potential was conducted

entities.name: PYROLYSIS
entities.description: The thermochemical decomposition of organic material at elevated temperatures in the absence of oxygen

entities.name: BAMBOO
entities.description: A fast-growing grass used as feedstock for biochar production

entities.name: CORN
entities.description: A cereal grain whose cobs are used as feedstock for biochar production

entities.name: COFFEE
entities.description: A plant whose husks are used as feedstock for biochar production

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to sustain plant growth

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input

entities.name: SURFACE MORPHOLOGY
entities.description: The physical structure and shape of the biochar surface

entities.name: BULK DENSITY
entities.description: The mass of biochar per unit volume

entities.name: VOLATILE MATTER
entities.description: The components of biochar that vaporize at high temperatures

entities.name: FIXED CARBON
entities.description: The carbon remaining in biochar after volatile matter is removed

entities.name: ASH CONTENT
entities.description: The inorganic residue remaining after the combustion of biochar

entities.name: PH VALUE
entities.description: The measure of acidity or alkalinity of the biochar

entities.name: NUTRIENT CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of essential nutrients present in the biochar

entities.name: CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of biochar to hold and exchange positively charged ions

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The carbon found in organic compounds within the biochar

entities.name: CALCIUM CARBONATE
entities.description: An inorganic compound found in biochar

entities.name: SOIL ACIDITY
entities.description: The state of soil having a low pH, which needs reclamation

entities.name: NUTRIENT-DEPLETED SOILS
entities.description: Soils lacking essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: DEGRADED SOILS
entities.description: Agricultural soils that have lost their productivity and quality

is the location where feedstock is available
